_meta.description: Character Sheet v1.1.0: progressive difficulty, confidence scoring, XP-as-buffer, per-pillar EMA

protocols._meta: Per-pillar protocol recommendations by tier. Surfaced in Daily Brief when pillar drops or needs attention.

Foundation: Fixed wake time ±30 min every day, including weekends — circadian anchor #1
Morning sunlight within 30 min of waking (10+ min, no sunglasses) — Huberman protocol
No screens 1 hour before bed — use NSDR audio or book instead
Bedroom temperature 65–67°F — set Eight Sleep accordingly
Momentum: NSDR protocol (10 min) at 2pm if prior night was <7 hours
No caffeine after 12pm — half-life is 5–6 hours
Evening wind-down routine: dim lights at 9pm, Eight Sleep cooling ramp
Discipline: Track sleep onset consistency — keep std dev under 30 min
Optimize deep sleep: limit alcohol (even 1 drink cuts deep sleep 20–40%)
Consider magnesium threonate 30 min before bed for sleep architecture
Mastery: Fine-tune Eight Sleep thermal profile based on sleep stage data
Monitor REM rebound patterns after sleep debt payback
Seasonal light exposure adjustments (earlier wake in summer, light therapy in winter)

Foundation: Start with 3 sessions per week — consistency over intensity
Walk 30+ min daily — lowest-hanging NEAT fruit
Track all activity in Strava — data creates accountability
Momentum: Build toward 150 min/week Zone 2 — the longevity threshold (Attia)
Add 1 strength session per week — sarcopenia prevention starts now
Progressive overload: increase volume or load 5–10% per week
Discipline: Polarize training: 80% Zone 2, 20% high intensity — avoid the middle
Monitor TSB (Training Stress Balance) — stay between -10 and +15
Add rucking: loaded walking builds Zone 2 + functional strength simultaneously
Mastery: Periodize in 4-week mesocycles with planned deload weeks
Track pace-at-HR over time as cardiovascular efficiency signal
Diversify movement patterns — aim for 4+ activity types per month

Foundation: Log everything in MacroFactor — even imperfect days. Data > perfection
Hit protein target (190g) — the non-negotiable macro during weight loss
Eat within designated window — structure prevents drift
Momentum: Distribute protein across 4+ meals (30g+ each) — MPS threshold matters (Norton)
Stay within ±10% of calorie target — consistency beats precision
Plan meals ahead — decision fatigue is the enemy of adherence
Discipline: Front-load protein: aim for 50g+ by noon to protect lean mass
Track weekend vs weekday adherence — close the gap
Use MacroFactor's adaptive algorithm — trust the math on expenditure
Mastery: Optimize micronutrient density — leverage The Weekly Plate for variety
Meal-specific glucose responses: identify your personal spike triggers (CGM)
Strategic refeeds during extended deficit phases to preserve metabolic rate

Foundation: Get baseline blood work (Function Health) — you can't manage what you don't measure
Track fasting glucose via CGM — morning reading is your metabolic canary
Monitor blood pressure weekly — silent risk factor
Momentum: Target time-in-range >85% (70–140 mg/dL) on CGM
Walk 10 min after meals — blunts glucose spikes 20–30% (Huberman)
Track ApoB trajectory across blood draws — the causal agent in ASCVD (Attia)
Discipline: Push toward time-in-range >90% — tighten the glucose variability band
Monitor waist circumference trend alongside scale weight (DEXA validation)
Optimize sleep to improve insulin sensitivity — they're tightly coupled (Patrick)
Mastery: Target ApoB <60 mg/dL — 20th percentile, optimal ASCVD risk reduction
Build personal postprandial glucose database — rank foods by your response
Quarterly blood work to validate lifestyle interventions are moving biomarkers

Foundation: Complete all T0 (non-negotiable) habits every day — the foundation is the routine
Journal at least 3x per week — process, don't accumulate
One deliberate stress-management practice daily (NSDR, breathwork, or walk)
Momentum: Extend vice streaks — each day compounds the neuroplasticity (Huberman)
State of Mind logging: capture valence data for pattern detection
Physiological sigh (double inhale + long exhale) for acute stress — 1 min protocol
Discipline: Journal every day — even 2 sentences. Consistency > depth
Review habit tier performance weekly — adjust T2 habits, protect T0
Identify avoidance patterns in journal entries — name the defense mechanism (Conti)
Mastery: Connect journal themes to objective data — what does the gap between feeling and metrics reveal?
Reduce T0 habit list only if current set is 95%+ for 30 days — earned simplification
Explore cognitive-behavioral patterns: intellectualization, displacement, isolation of affect

Foundation: Log at least 1 meaningful social interaction per week via journal or contact tool
Reach out to Tom or a friend proactively once per week — don't wait to be contacted
Notice social mentions in journal — are you writing about people or just metrics?
Momentum: Aim for 3+ meaningful social interactions per week (Murthy)
Diversify social connections — don't rely on one person for all social needs
Use Brittany accountability to strengthen partnership engagement
Discipline: Schedule social time like training — it's not less important, just less urgent
Track contact frequency — are you maintaining breadth or narrowing?
Quality over quantity: one deep conversation > five surface interactions
Mastery: Invest in 2–3 relationships that provide mutual growth and accountability
Social connection affects every other pillar — treat it as infrastructure, not luxury
Reflect on relationship patterns in journal: giving vs. taking, vulnerability vs. guardedness

Foundation: Focus on data completeness — log everything, even bad days. Gaps are worse than bad data
Minimize weekend-weekday variance — the gap reveals your real routine
One priority per week: don't try to fix all pillars at once
Momentum: Build minimum viable days — even your worst day should hit basic thresholds
Cross-pillar awareness: notice which pillar you sacrifice first under pressure
Reduce variance before increasing averages — stability enables growth
Discipline: Aim for 'no pillar below 30' on any given day — protect the floor
Use travel and disruption as consistency tests, not excuses
Review weekly: which pillar was most volatile? That's your leverage point
Mastery: Target Alignment Bonus (all pillars 41+) — this is the meta-achievement
Consistency at this level is identity, not discipline — you've become the person
Mentor others: Tom, Brittany, future open-source users — teaching deepens mastery
